$comment: AFL Fantasy per-season tier (Premier League / D-League) membership and champions — the single source of truth for the all-play side competition. MFL does NOT store tier membership (its all-play export returns one 24-team list with no tier markers), so it lives here. Keyed by year. `membership` maps franchiseId -> tier as teams competed THAT season; `champions` records the #1 of each tier (slugs premier-league / dleague-champion). Membership for the latest completed season is seeded from afl.config.json; future-year membership and computed champions are written by scripts/compute-afl-tier-movement.mjs (promotion/relegation roll-forward, idempotent). 2018-2025 membership was recovered from the league skin's per-year grouping scripts (mfl.football/afl-fantasy.com/assets/js/premierleague-YYYY.js) which hardcode each season's Premier/D-League rosters; those years' champions remain hand-entered. The competition began in 2017 — the year after the last AFL Cup (2015-2016) — as ONE combined 24-team all-play table; its top 12 formed the 2018 Premier League, the bottom 12 the D-League. Split-tier seasons ran 2018+ and every split season 2018-2025 has a skin grouping script (the ONE source of truth for membership, per Brandon); 2017 has none because one table needs no grouping. The 2025 entry was originally mis-seeded from an afl.config snapshot and is corrected to match premierleague-2025.js. Movement rule: owners who JOIN the league always start in the D-League regardless of which tier their franchise slot held — a turnover year relegates correspondingly fewer Premier League teams, so the pure constitution roll-forward cannot reconstruct those years.

2018.membershipSource: recovered from the league skin's official per-year grouping script (mfl.football/afl-fantasy.com/assets/js/premierleague-2018.js), the same script that renders the Premier/D-League tables on the MFL site — verified: cutoff-week all-play within these tiers reproduces the hand-recorded champions (Premier 0014, D-League 0003)
2018.championsSource: manual:league-awards (independently reproduced by computed all-play within the skin-js tiers)

2019.membershipSource: recovered from the league skin's official per-year grouping script (mfl.football/afl-fantasy.com/assets/js/premierleague-2019.js), the same script that renders the Premier/D-League tables on the MFL site — verified: cutoff-week all-play within these tiers reproduces the hand-recorded champions (Premier 0001, D-League 0002)
2019.championsSource: manual:league-awards (independently reproduced by computed all-play within the skin-js tiers)
